data.title: Elon Musk's Tesla Invested $2 Billion Into SpaceX, Q1 2026 Earnings Show
data.description: Elon Musk's Tesla Inc. (NASDAQ: TSLA) has deepened its connection with SpaceX through a recent investment in the commercial space flight company, alongside a collaboration with Terafab. The quarterly earnings report revealed a significant $2 billion investment in SpaceX, leading to a $2 billion charge in Tesla's report attributed to its "SpaceX equity investment." The partnership with SpaceX is seen as crucial by Tesla, with the company emphasizing the importance of expanding manufacturing operations to include semiconductor fabrication. This move aims to secure a steady chip supply and enhance resilience. Furthermore, Tesla highlighted the joint endeavor with SpaceX to construct a mega chip fab that will vertically integrate logic, memory, and cutting-edge technology.

data.title: Deepening China-Germany Collaboration: Mom's Garden Showcases New Collagen Product at CICPE
data.description: A new bone collagen product from Mom's Garden was introduced at the 6th China International Consumer Products Expo (CICPE) in Hainan on April 13, 2026. The German brand showcased its innovative product focusing on bone health at the event. This was part of the expo's grand opening and marked an important debut for the brand in the Chinese market.

data.title: Goldman Sachs Alternatives Invests $50 Million in BLP Digital, Betting on Agentic AI for Core Finance Processes
data.description: A notable development in Switzerland as BLP Digital, a top player in Agentic AI-powered enterprise resource planning automation, revealed securing a $50 million investment from Growth Equity at Goldman Sachs Alternatives. The technology offered by BLP Digital plays a pivotal role in diminishing manual efforts in essential finance tasks like Accounts Payable, resulting in quicker closing times and notable impacts on cash flow and working capital. The company's seamless integration into current ERP systems further enhances its appeal.

data.title: Microsoft Reportedly Eyed Cursor Before SpaceX's $60 Billion Deal— But Chose Not To Proceed
data.description: US tech company Microsoft Corp. (NASDAQ: MSFT) had shown interest in acquiring AI coding startup Cursor before SpaceX offered a $60 billion deal. Microsoft's CEO Satya Nadella aimed to enhance its AI tools in the expanding AI market, but decided not to proceed with a bid, as per CNBC. Neither Microsoft nor Cursor responded to Benzinga's request for comments. This development surfaces amid Microsoft's strategies in the tech industry, with significant implications for the AI sector. The potential acquisition and subsequent decision convey the competitive landscape in the tech and AI sectors.
